canto_title: The Frozen Landscape of Cocytus and the Encounters with Traitors

scenes.summary: Dante introduces the difficulty of his task and invokes the Muses for help.
Dante describes the descent into the bottom of the universe and receives a warning to watch his step.
Dante observes the immense frozen lake and compares its cold to the great rivers of the world.
Dante describes the distorted and frozen appearance of the souls trapped in the ice.
Dante encounters two souls whose heads are pressed so tightly together that their hair is mingled.
A third, partially deaf sinner identifies the two previous souls and provides his own name.
Dante describes the movement through the crowd and an accidental step onto a sinner's face.
The sinner who was stepped on rebukes Dante, referencing the battle of Montaperti.
Dante and the scolding sinner engage in a sharp and aggressive exchange of words.
Dante reacts to the sinner's refusal to identify himself by physically pulling his hair.
A new sinner interrupts the struggle to mock the first sinner's behavior.
The sinner Bocca provides a list of other famous traitors frozen in the ice.
Dante observes two more souls trapped in a pit, one biting the other's temple.
Dante questions the biting sinner about the animosity he shows toward his companion.
